2603.19191.title: OS-Themis: A Scalable Critic Framework for Generalist GUI Rewards
2603.19191.paper_summary: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has the potential to improve the robustness of GUI agents in stochastic environments, yet training is highly sensitive to the quality of the reward function. Existing reward approaches struggle to achieve both scalability and performance. To address this, we propose OS-Themis, a scalable and accurate multi-agent critic framework. Unlike a single judge, OS-Themis decomposes trajectories into verifiable milestones to isolate critical evidence for decision making and employs a review mechanism to strictly audit the evidence chain before making the final verdict. To facilitate evaluation, we further introduce OmniGUIRewardBench (OGRBench), a holistic cross-platform benchmark for GUI outcome rewards, where all evaluated models achieve their best performance under OS-Themis. Extensive experiments on AndroidWorld show that OS-Themis yields a 10.3% improvement when used to support online RL training, and a 6.9% gain when used for trajectory validation and filtering in the self-training loop, highlighting its potential to drive agent evolution.
2603.19191.paper_summary_zh: 強化學習 (RL) 有潛力提升 GUI 代理在隨機環境中的穩健性，但訓練對獎勵函數的質量非常敏感。現有的獎勵方法在可擴展性和性能之間難以取得平衡。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了 OS-Themis，一個可擴展且準確的多代理評價框架。與單一評審不同，OS-Themis 將軌跡分解為可驗證的里程碑，以隔離決策所需的關鍵證據，並採用審查機制在做出最終裁決之前嚴格審核證據鏈。為了促進評估，我們進一步引入了 OmniGUIRewardBench (OGRBench)，這是一個全面的跨平台 GUI 結果獎勵基準，所有評估的模型在 OS-Themis 下都達到了最佳性能。在 AndroidWorld 上的廣泛實驗顯示，使用 OS-Themis 支持在線 RL 訓練時，性能提高了 10.3%，而在自我訓練循環中用於軌跡驗證和過濾時，性能提高了 6.9%，突顯了其推動代理進化的潛力。
2603.19191.author: Zehao Li et.al.
2603.19191.authors: Zehao Li, Zhenyu Wu, Yibo Zhao, Bowen Yang, Jingjing Xie, Zhaoyang Liu, Zhoumianze Liu, Kaiming Jin, Jianze Liang, Zonglin Li, Feng Wu, Bowen Zhou, Zun Wang, Zichen Ding
